title: Distributed Systems
description: Coordination, consistency and failure handling across multiple services and nodes.

questions.question: What does it mean for an operation to be idempotent, and why does at-least-once message delivery force idempotency onto consumers?
Why is 'exactly-once delivery' considered practically impossible in distributed messaging, and what do systems do instead to get exactly-once *processing*?
An order service must update its own database AND publish an 'order created' event. Why is doing these as two separate steps dangerous, and how does the outbox pattern fix it?
Compare two-phase commit and the saga pattern for a transaction spanning multiple services. Why do microservice architectures usually prefer sagas?
Why can't you trust wall-clock timestamps to order events across machines, and what alternatives exist (e.g. logical clocks)?
What is leader election and why do distributed systems need it? Sketch how a system like ZooKeeper, etcd or Raft-based clusters make it safe.
Design a distributed rate limiter for an API served by 20 instances. Where does the counting state live, and what accuracy vs latency trade-offs do different designs make?
